info.title: Datadog API
info.description:
The Datadog API is an HTTP REST API that allows you to programmatically integrate with Datadog's monitoring, alerting, and observability platform. Use resource-oriented URLs and standard HTTP methods to manage monitors, dashboards, metrics, logs, incidents, SLOs, synthetics tests, and more.

Datadog supports three authentication methods:
- **API Key** (`DD-API-KEY` header) — required for all requests; identifies your organization.
- **Application Key** (`DD-APPLICATION-KEY` header) — required for most endpoints; grants a user-scoped access context.
- **OAuth 2.0** — Authorization Code flow with PKCE for third-party integrations and Marketplace apps.

Both v1 and v2 endpoints are served from the same base URL. The API returns JSON and uses standard HTTP status codes.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: Validate API and application keys

tags.name: Monitors
tags.description: Create, edit, delete, and manage monitors that alert on metric thresholds, integration statuses, and more

tags.name: Dashboards
tags.description: Create and manage dashboards and dashboard lists for visualizing metrics, traces, and logs

tags.name: Metrics
tags.description: Query, submit, and manage metric data, tag configurations, and metadata

tags.name: Events
tags.description: Post events to the event stream and query existing events

tags.name: Logs
tags.description: Search, list, and send log entries to Datadog

tags.name: Synthetics
tags.description: Create and manage synthetic monitoring tests (API and browser tests)

tags.description: Create, edit, and manage SLOs based on monitors or metrics

get.description: Check if the API key in the `DD-API-KEY` header is valid.

post.description: Validate the application key in the `DD-APPLICATION-KEY` header.

get.description: Get all monitor details. Supports filtering by name, tags, and monitor type.

parameters.name: group_states
parameters.description: Filter by comma-separated list of group states (e.g., `alert`, `warn`, `no data`).

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Filter monitors by comma-separated list of tags.

parameters.name: monitor_tags
parameters.description: Filter monitors by comma-separated list of monitor tags.

parameters.name: id_offset
parameters.description: Return monitors with IDs greater than or equal to this value for pagination.

post.description: Create a monitor with the specified type, query, name, and options.

get.description: Get details for a single monitor by ID.

parameters.name: group_states
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of group states to include.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Delete the monitor even if it is referenced by other resources.

post.description: Mute all monitors globally.
Unmute all monitors globally.
Mute the specified monitor.

get.description: Search and filter monitors using a query string.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Monitor search query following the monitor search syntax.

get.description: Get all dashboards. Paginated with optional filtering.

parameters.name: filter[shared]
parameters.description: When true, return only shared dashboards.

parameters.name: filter[deleted]
parameters.description: When true, return only recently deleted dashboards.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of the queried time period as a POSIX timestamp.

parameters.description: End of the queried time period as a POSIX timestamp.

post.description: Submit metrics to Datadog. Compression with gzip or deflate is supported and recommended.

parameters.name: Content-Encoding
parameters.description: HTTP header for payload compression (`gzip` or `deflate`).

413.description: Payload too large (max 500,000 metrics per request, 512KB uncompressed).

get.description: Get the list of actively reporting metrics from a given time until now.

parameters.name: filter[configured]
parameters.description: Filter metrics with or without tag configurations.

parameters.name: filter[tags_configured]
parameters.description: Filter tag configuration by configured tags.

parameters.name: filter[queried]
parameters.description: Filter metrics queried in the last specified number of hours.

parameters.name: window[seconds]
parameters.description: Number of seconds of lookback for actively reporting metrics.

get.description: Get metadata about a specific metric.
Returns the tag configuration for a given metric name.

parameters.name: ddtags

get.description: Redirect users to Datadog's consent page to authorize your application. Supports PKCE with `code_challenge` and `code_challenge_method` parameters.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: The client ID of the OAuth application.

parameters.name: redirect_uri
parameters.description: The URI to redirect to after authorization.

post.description: Exchange an authorization code for an access token, or refresh an existing access token.
Revoke an access or refresh token.

apiKeyAuth.name: DD-API-KEY
apiKeyAuth.description: Datadog API key. Required for all API requests. Obtain from your Datadog organization settings at https://app.datadoghq.com/organization-settings/api-keys.

appKeyAuth.name: DD-APPLICATION-KEY
appKeyAuth.description: Datadog Application key. Required alongside the API key for most endpoints. Provides a user-scoped access context. Obtain from https://app.datadoghq.com/organization-settings/application-keys.

datadog_oauth.description: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow with PKCE for third-party integrations and Datadog Marketplace applications. Create a confidential OAuth client in the Datadog Developer Platform. Access tokens are short-lived; use refresh tokens to obtain new ones. Include the access token in the `Authorization: Bearer {token}` header.

scopes.synthetics_read: Read synthetics test configurations and results
scopes.synthetics_global_variable_write: Create and edit synthetics global variables
scopes.synthetics_private_location_write: Create and edit synthetics private locations
scopes.user_access_manage: Manage user access and RBAC roles

BadRequest.description: Bad request — invalid parameters or malformed request body.

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized — authentication credentials are missing or invalid.

Forbidden.description: Forbidden — not authorized to perform the requested action.

NotFound.description: Not found — the requested resource does not exist.

TooManyRequests.description: Too many requests — rate limit exceeded. Check `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and `X-RateLimit-Reset` headers.

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error response from the Datadog API.

tags.description: Tags associated with the monitor.

priority.description: Integer from 1 (high) to 5 (low) indicating alert severity.

restricted_roles.description: Roles allowed to edit this monitor.

timeout_h.description: Auto-resolve timeout in hours (0 to disable).

silenced.description: Map of scopes to their mute end timestamps.

no_data_timeframe.description: Minutes before a no-data alert triggers.

require_full_window.description: Whether the monitor needs a full evaluation window.

new_host_delay.description: Seconds to wait for new hosts before evaluation.

MonitorThresholdWindows.description: Alerting time window options for anomaly and outlier monitors.

trigger_window.description: Describes how long an anomalous metric must be to trigger an alert (e.g., `last_5m`).

recovery_window.description: Describes how long a metric must be normal to recover.

MonitorCreateRequest.description: Request body to create a monitor.

MonitorUpdateRequest.description: Request body to update a monitor.

end.description: POSIX timestamp for when the mute should end.

widgets.description: List of widgets on the dashboard.

notify_list.description: List of handles for users to notify on changes.

definition.description: Widget definition object. Structure varies by widget type.

layout.description: Widget layout for free layout dashboards.

name.description: The name of the dashboard list.

interval.description: Interval of the metric in seconds (required for rate/count metrics).

text.description: The body of the event (max 4000 characters). Supports markdown.

aggregation_key.description: An arbitrary string used to aggregate events.

query.description: Search query following Datadog log search syntax.

id.description: The name of the log-based metric.

end.description: POSIX timestamp when the mute should end.

tags.description: Map of tags to host names.
List of tags to apply (e.g., `env:production`).

scope.description: The scope to which the downtime applies (e.g., `env:prod`).

name.description: Name of the new child organization (max 32 characters).

time.description: Global time frame for the notebook.

key.description: Full key value (only returned on creation).

redirect_uri.description: Must match the redirect URI used in the authorization request.

code_verifier.description: PKCE code verifier (required if code_challenge was used during authorization).

access_token.description: The access token for API requests.

refresh_token.description: Token to obtain a new access token.

created_at.description: POSIX timestamp when the token was created.

token.description: The access or refresh token to revoke.

token_type_hint.description: Hint about the token type being revoked.

Creator.description: The user who created the resource.
